title: The Innovators: How a Group of Hackers, Geniuses, and Geeks Created the Digital Revolution
rationale: The definitive single-volume popular history of the digital revolution written by one of America's leading biographers. Chosen as Book #1 for the history-of-technology leaf because it establishes the subcategory's defining thesis — that collaboration, not solitary genius, drives technological progress — while tracing computing from Ada Lovelace (1843) to Google and Wikipedia. Unconventional for the genre in its deliberate recovery of erased women contributors and its measured celebration of government-funded research alongside private enterprise.
